title: 10 facts about Cubans in the U.S.
summary_en: Millions of people living in the U.S. trace their origins to Cuba. They make up the third-largest Hispanic origin group in the U.S., after Mexicans and Puerto Ricans.
title_cn: [待翻译] 10 facts about Cubans in the U.S.

title: Moms, Coaches, Doctors, Entrepreneurs: Who Are America’s Health and Wellness Influencers?
summary_en: Half of U.S. adults under 50 say they get health and wellness information from social media influencers or podcasts. About 4 in 10 of these influencers describe themselves as health care professionals; coaches and entrepreneurs are almost as common.
title_cn: [待翻译] Moms, Coaches, Doctors, Entrepreneurs: Who Are America’s Health and Wellness Influencers?

title: 71% of U.S. homeowners say their home insurance costs have gone up
summary_en: The two reasons homeowners cite most often are insurance companies wanting to make more money and the costs of repairing and rebuilding.
title_cn: [待翻译] 71% of U.S. homeowners say their home insurance costs have gone up

title: Many Americans hold utility companies responsible for their rising home energy bills
summary_en: Three-quarters of U.S. adults say their home energy costs have gone up in recent years, including 42% who say these costs have gone up a lot.
title_cn: [待翻译] Many Americans hold utility companies responsible for their rising home energy bills

title: How question wording affects polling on the morality of homosexuality
summary_en: Americans largely don’t distinguish between the morality of “homosexuality” and “homosexual behavior,” though some subgroups may, according to a new survey experiment.
title_cn: [待翻译] How question wording affects polling on the morality of homosexuality
